summary: Reviewed the Sunday walk-in launch changes for module boundaries, payment/data-flow ownership, migration fit, and legacy-path preservation. The main risks are around deposit state ownership being split across wrappers and follow-up writes, plus PayPal order reuse bypassing the new canonical amount contract. Overall the central helper direction is sound, but several integration points still let legacy or stale payment state leak through.
appears_sound: The deposit threshold/rate is centralized in `src/lib/table-bookings/deposit.ts:13`, and `requiresDeposit` correctly preserves waiver semantics before applying the 10+ rule.

PayPal capture locks the captured amount in the same booking update as payment confirmation in `src/app/api/external/table-bookings/[id]/paypal/capture-order/route.ts:155`, which is the right ownership point for that flow.
The public booking route deliberately passes `p_sunday_lunch: false` in `src/app/api/table-bookings/route.ts:245`, keeping new public bookings out of the legacy Sunday-lunch booking type.
The canonical-deposit behavior is covered directly in `tests/lib/table-bookings/deposit.test.ts:35`, including locked, stored, computed, below-threshold, and waived cases.

findings.description: Migration C wraps the legacy core booking function instead of replacing its deposit decision, then returns the legacy result unchanged for parties below 10. If the legacy function still applies the old Sunday/7+ deposit side effects, `_core` flows will continue using the old rule despite the migration's contract.
findings.evidence: `create_table_booking_v05_core` delegates to `create_table_booking_v05_core_sunday_deposit_legacy` at `supabase/migrations/20260509000016_patch_v05_core_threshold.sql:51` and returns `v_result` unchanged below 10 at `supabase/migrations/20260509000016_patch_v05_core_threshold.sql:65`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: This would be harmless only if the delegated legacy function had already been changed to stop creating pending-payment deposits for Sunday lunch or 7-9 covers.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the body of `create_table_booking_v05_core_sunday_deposit_legacy` or run RPC tests for Sunday lunch with 2 covers and regular bookings with 7-9 covers.

findings.description: The PayPal create-order route returns an existing `paypal_deposit_order_id` before recalculating the canonical deposit amount. A booking whose party size or stored/locked deposit changed after order creation can reuse and capture a stale PayPal order, then lock the stale captured amount as authoritative.
findings.evidence: The route exits on `booking.paypal_deposit_order_id` at `src/app/api/external/table-bookings/[id]/paypal/create-order/route.ts:60`, before `getCanonicalDeposit` is called at `src/app/api/external/table-bookings/[id]/paypal/create-order/route.ts:67`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If PayPal order IDs are always cleared whenever deposit inputs change, this path may not become stale, but that invalidation is not shown in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check all party-size/deposit mutation paths for clearing or replacing `paypal_deposit_order_id`, or verify the PayPal order amount against canonical amount before reuse.

findings.description: Deposit locking is not transactionally owned by the payment-confirmation operation in the cash and Stripe paths. A failed follow-up lock write leaves a completed/confirmed booking without `deposit_amount_locked`, undermining the canonical locked-amount invariant.
findings.evidence: The cash path logs `cashLockError` without blocking after the RPC confirms payment at `src/app/api/foh/bookings/route.ts:1242`, and the Stripe webhook separately writes or skips the lock after the completion RPC at `src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts:492`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Manual reconciliation or a later audit could repair these rows, but the code itself allows the inconsistent state to be committed.
findings.what_would_confirm: Move the lock amount into the confirming RPC/update transaction, or add a database-level guard/test proving completed table deposits cannot remain unlocked.

findings.description: The backend still preserves a staff-explicit legacy Sunday-lunch path, but the FOH modal hard-disables the only visible `sunday_lunch` input. That creates a UI/API contract mismatch and appears to break the stated legacy admin backfill workflow.
findings.evidence: The checkbox is rendered with `disabled` at `src/app/(authenticated)/table-bookings/foh/components/FohCreateBookingModal.tsx:415`, while FOH creation still retains `effectiveSundayLunch`/legacy persistence behavior in `src/app/api/foh/bookings/route.ts:1049`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: There may be another legacy-admin entry surface outside the pack, or the intended decision may be to retire UI-based legacy creation entirely.
findings.what_would_confirm: Confirm whether staff must be able to create/backfill legacy `booking_type='sunday_lunch'` bookings through FOH after this change.

findings.description: The Sunday business-hours change is intentionally parked in `scripts/one-off` rather than a migration, so fresh environments and `supabase db push` will not reproduce the launched service window. This creates deployment drift unless the rollout explicitly accepts manual post-deploy state.
findings.evidence: The script says the update is local-only and should later be captured inside a tracked migration at `scripts/one-off/2026-04-28-sunday-hours-13-18.sql:11`, but the changed migrations do not include the business-hours update.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The staged rollout may intentionally require a manual production toggle before a later migration records the steady state.
findings.what_would_confirm: Owner sign-off that the one-off script is the deployment source of truth for this wave, or a tracked migration containing the final Sunday hours update.

empty_categories: No circular dependency introduced by the new deposit helper; `src/lib/table-bookings/deposit.ts` is a leaf utility in the pack.
No broad shared mutable client state introduced; the changed UI state remains local to the FOH create-booking hook/modal.
No destructive schema change found in the pack; Migration A only adds `deposit_amount_locked` and performs targeted backfills/updates.
No raw database access was moved into client components; client changes continue to go through existing API/route boundaries.
